population: Device-validation and general physical-activity contexts; not a participant intervention study
intervention: Standardized evaluation recommendations for wearable-sensor reliability sensitivity and validity
comparator: Heterogeneous prior experimental and statistical assessment approaches
component_dose: External internal or derived wearable outputs remain in their original quantity and unit; contextual provenance includes device model sensor placement activity environment sampling protocol firmware or algorithm criterion method and timestamps
component_order: NOT_APPLICABLE
outcome_measure: Reliability sensitivity validity and trustworthiness of wearable-sensor data
instrument_protocol: Full-text methodological guidance covering device experimental and statistical considerations; every validation claim remains device outcome activity population and protocol specific
effect_estimate: Provides standardized assessment recommendations rather than a pooled device-performance effect
uncertainty: Device-specific measurement error and transfer uncertainty must be established before interpreting change; no universal threshold reported
limitations: Method guidance rather than validation of any TRAINORACLE device metric; no direct youth middle-distance population; device validity cannot be transferred across outcomes models or protocols and cannot create a fatigue readiness safety or prescription state

population: Adult field- and court-sport athletes across 21 included studies
sex: MIXED_ACROSS_STUDIES; exact distribution not reported in abstract
competitive_level: Mainly team-sport athletes; exact levels heterogeneous
intervention: Single-item self-reports of muscle soreness fatigue sleep quality stress mood and other wellbeing dimensions
comparator: Modifiable training-load measures reported in each study
component_dose: Internal or contextual self-report item scores retain the named construct scale anchors prompt timing and reporter; external and derived training-load comparators retain their own method and unit and remain separate
component_order: Each single-item construct remains a separate observation; no item or load measure is merged into one whole-athlete score
outcome_measure: Associations between named wellbeing items and training-load metrics; measurement-property evidence
instrument_protocol: Systematic review of PubMed Scopus and SPORTDiscus from inception to March 2019; 21 studies with at least 7 days of observation; narrative synthesis
time_horizon: At least 7 days in each included study; exact ranges heterogeneous
time_points: Repeated study-specific self-report and load observations
effect_estimate: Relationships ranged from no association to very large; in studies with the most observations associations were predominantly trivial to moderate and were largely negative
uncertainty: Narrative heterogeneous evidence with no pooled universal relationship reported
individual_response: Individual-level measurement properties and response rules were not established
limitations: Adult team sports rather than youth distance runners; association remained unclear and may not be expected or useful; weak measurement-property evidence; named items must not be collapsed with objective load or each other into a single fatigue readiness recovery or safety score

population: United States HIPAA covered-entity context; not a sport-science population
intervention: HIPAA de-identification through Expert Determination or Safe Harbor
comparator: Identifiable protected health information and the alternative HHS-recognized de-identification method
component_dose: Contextual provenance includes source data category transformation or suppression method responsible qualified expert or covered entity decision date method version retained identifiers and release purpose; no training-load unit is created
component_order: Expert Determination and Safe Harbor are alternative methods; transformations form versioned lineage and do not overwrite source identity
outcome_measure: De-identification method documentation and residual re-identification risk
instrument_protocol: Official HHS guidance for the HIPAA Privacy Rule; Expert Determination applies accepted statistical and scientific principles and documents methods and results; Safe Harbor removes specified identifiers and requires no actual knowledge that remaining information identifies an individual
effect_estimate: HHS recognizes two de-identification methods and describes residual re-identification risk as very small rather than zero under the applicable standard
uncertainty: Residual risk is context dependent and not eliminated; no universal numeric safe threshold is provided
limitations: US HIPAA guidance is not a universal global privacy rule or sport-effect study; de-identification does not grant training fatigue readiness medical or prescription meaning; a qualified privacy decision and purpose-specific controls remain necessary

intervention: Multidisciplinary internal and external training-load monitoring framework
comparator: Heterogeneous applied monitoring methods
component_dose: External work; internal response; method-specific derived values; contextual facts; original units retained
component_order: NOT_APPLICABLE
outcome_measure: Internal and external load families; method heterogeneity
instrument_protocol: 2016 Doha expert conference consensus synthesis; method and unit provenance required for product use
effect_estimate: Consensus establishes separate measurable families but supplies no pooled effect estimate
limitations: Consensus rather than validation; no youth middle-distance directness; no universal total; no fatigue or readiness inference

intervention: Two-component internal and external training-load construct
comparator: Prior and potentially ambiguous load definitions
component_dose: External work and internal response retained as different families in their reported units
component_order: NOT_APPLICABLE
outcome_measure: Conceptual distinction between external load and internal response
instrument_protocol: Conceptual commentary; operational method identity and provenance required before comparison
effect_estimate: Definitions require clarification to avoid misinterpretation
limitations: Commentary rather than validation; exact operational definitions not verified beyond abstract; not youth-runner specific; external work cannot imply internal response or readiness

population: Cycling participants and basketball players; exact composition not verified
intervention: Whole-session RPE multiplied by session duration
comparator: Heart-rate-based training-load method
component_dose: Internal response: session RPE on named scale; external duration in minutes; derived sRPE load in method-specific arbitrary units; HR-derived comparator in its own arbitrary units
component_order: Whole session only; no component allocation protocol
outcome_measure: Relationship and absolute difference between session-RPE and HR-based scores
instrument_protocol: Steady-state and interval cycling plus basketball; prompt scale timing duration source and formula version must remain in provenance
time_points: Session completion; exact collection timing not verified from abstract
effect_estimate: Relationships were consistent across exercise types but absolute session-RPE scores were significantly greater than HR-method scores
limitations: HR comparator is not a complete criterion for every stress; sample detail absent from abstract; not youth runners; no validated parent-to-leaf or component allocation; correlation does not permit cross-method conversion summation fatigue or readiness inference

sex: BOTH; exact counts not reported in abstract
intervention: CR10 session RPE and differential leg and breathlessness RPE collected at 0 15 and 30 minutes
comparator: Heart-rate criteria and within-session timing comparisons
component_dose: Internal response: CR10 RPE points; external duration retained separately; derived sRPE arbitrary units only under a complete named formula
component_order: Whole-session readings at three response times; no leaf allocation
outcome_measure: sRPE; leg RPE; breathlessness RPE; correlations with heart-rate criteria
instrument_protocol: Treadmill familiarization plus regular training; CR10 scale; end and response timestamps required; timing protocol is part of value provenance
effect_estimate: At 30 versus 0 minutes sRPE -26.5%; leg RPE -20.5%; breathlessness -38.9%; correlations with HR criteria largest at completion r=.74-.89; all timings judged valid
uncertainty: 90% limits +/-5.5% for sRPE; +/-5.6% for leg RPE; +/-7.4% for breathlessness
limitations: Small short study; sex-specific results and maturity not reported; 800/1500 composition not reported; immediate and 30-minute protocols cannot be silently pooled; no fatigue readiness or clearance meaning

population: Athletes across sex age competition level and sport
intervention: Foster session-RPE method using modified CR10
comparator: Validity reliability and internal-consistency evidence across included studies
component_dose: Internal response: named modified-CR10 rating; external duration and units retained; derived sRPE formula-specific arbitrary units
component_order: Whole-session method; component allocation not validated
outcome_measure: Session-RPE validity reliability internal consistency and influencing factors
instrument_protocol: Review of 36 validation and reliability studies; scale prompt collection timing duration convention and population context must remain in provenance
effect_estimate: Broad validity reliability and internal consistency reported across sports and ages
uncertainty: Not pooled into one effect estimate in this extraction
limitations: English-language restriction; broad heterogeneity; low youth middle-distance directness; correlations do not establish numerical equivalence; no component split fatigue or readiness authority

population: Recreational systematically training athletes across sports; sample size not reported in abstract
intervention: Borg CR10 and Borg 6-20 whole-session RPE after randomized matched interval sessions
comparator: Heart-rate reserve and cross-scale comparison
component_dose: External dose: six 30-minute sessions at 50% 75% or 85% peak power; internal response: CR10 points and Borg 6-20 points; derived duration products remain scale-specific arbitrary units
component_order: Whole-session matched sessions; no component allocation
outcome_measure: CR10 sRPE; Borg 6-20 sRPE; heart-rate reserve
instrument_protocol: Randomized 30-minute interval sessions; named scale anchors collection timing peak-power prescription and formula provenance required
effect_estimate: CR10 and Borg 6-20 correlated r=.90; correlations with HR reserve r=.76 and r=.69; absolute values differed
limitations: Adult or mixed-sport indirectness; controlled intervals; high correlation is not absolute agreement; no raw pooling conversion component allocation fatigue or readiness inference

population: Patients through elite athletes across exercise modes
intervention: Historical whole-session session-RPE method
comparator: Earlier and later collection-timing practices across the literature
component_dose: Internal response: average RPE of entire session on named scale; external session duration retained; derived sRPE in formula-specific arbitrary units
component_order: Global sRPE belongs to parent session; ordered leaves retain only their own observed facts; no proportional parent-to-leaf split
separation_hours: Original method about 0.5 hours post-bout; wider intervals discussed
actual_interval_hours: Original method about 0.5 hours post-bout; wider intervals discussed
outcome_measure: Entire-session average RPE and collection timing
instrument_protocol: Historical narrative review; prompt scale anchors session duration source timing and formula version required
time_points: About 30 minutes post-bout originally; wider intervals in later literature
effect_estimate: Defines sRPE as intensity or average RPE of an entire session and reports temporal robustness in wider literature
limitations: Narrative and developer-authored; timing summary does not erase direct timing differences; no validated component allocation; parent and leaves must never both count the same global sRPE; no external-detail fatigue or readiness inference

intervention: Individualized TRIMPi based on each runner's heart-rate and lactate profile
comparator: Average group-weighted training impulse method
component_dose: Internal response: heart rate and blood lactate profile; external duration retained; derived TRIMPi in method-specific arbitrary units; 5 km and 10 km performance retained in original units
component_order: Whole-session or recorded-bout TRIMPi; no cross-method component split
outcome_measure: Weekly TRIMPi; speed at 2 and 4 mmol/L; 5 km and 10 km performance
instrument_protocol: Individual treadmill HR-lactate testing plus versioned individual weighting; threshold profile and formula lineage required
time_points: Weekly load and pre-post performance or physiological outcomes
effect_estimate: Weekly TRIMPi related to speed change at 2 mmol/L r=.87 and 4 mmol/L r=.74 and to 5 km and 10 km performance; group-average methods showed no significant relationships
individual_response: Eight runners; individual estimates not reported in abstract
limitations: Very small adult recreational sample; association is not method agreement youth validation or causality; TRIMPi cannot be relabeled generic TRIMP or converted to other arbitrary-unit methods; no fatigue readiness inference

intervention: Parallel sRPE heart-rate TRIMP and IMU plus peak-ground-reaction-force cumulative biomechanical load
comparator: Comparison across endurance submaximal and interval outdoor sessions
component_dose: Internal response: whole-session RPE points; physiological derived load: named HR-TRIMP arbitrary units; biomechanical derived load: IMU and peak-GRF method-specific units; external session facts retained separately
component_order: Parent session holds global sRPE; modality and ordered session components retained; measures remain adjacent and non-additive
outcome_measure: sRPE; TRIMP; cumulative biomechanical load; session-type differences; correlations
instrument_protocol: Outdoor sessions with named devices sensors force derivation HR formula RPE protocol timestamps and method versions required
effect_estimate: sRPE distinguished session types while TRIMP and biomechanical load did not; correlations r=.49 sRPE/TRIMP r=.25 sRPE/biomechanical and r=.35 biomechanical/TRIMP
limitations: Small adult sample; criterion adequacy uncertain; no youth or maturity evidence; three session types; no validated parent-to-leaf allocation for composite work; weak-to-moderate correlations prohibit summing these arbitrary units or collapsing them to fatigue readiness or one currency

intervention: Session-RPE across explosive power endurance strength ergometer and functional training
comparator: Banister TRIMP or total weight lifted depending on modality
component_dose: Internal response: session RPE points; external strength dose: total weight lifted in mass units; physiological derived load: Banister TRIMP arbitrary units; duration retained; each method remains distinct
component_order: Whole-session sRPE with modality-specific comparator; composite strength/endurance leaves remain ordered and are not assigned an ergometer conversion
outcome_measure: sRPE test-retest; ICC; sRPE-TRIMP correlation; sRPE-total-weight relationship by modality
instrument_protocol: Longitudinal modality-specific protocol; named RPE scale timing duration formula HR method and lifted-mass units required
time_points: Typical sessions across four broad modalities
effect_estimate: Ergometer test-retest r about .758 and ICC .755; sRPE-TRIMP r=.811 in 6 km x 3 ergometer work and r=.258 in functional training
limitations: Rowing rather than running; criterion changed by modality; abstract lists five typical sessions within four broad modalities; no youth directness; composite sessions cannot inherit ergometer conversion or collapse to fatigue readiness

population: Adults spanning Fitzpatrick skin tones 1-6; sample size not reported in abstract
intervention: Optical heart-rate measurement from six consumer and research wearables
comparator: Electrocardiography across rest and activities
component_dose: Internal response proxy: heart rate in beats per minute; contextual provenance: device model activity condition skin-tone category firmware or algorithm version and missingness; no substitution for other outcomes
component_order: NOT_APPLICABLE
outcome_measure: Heart-rate error by device activity and skin tone; device missing values
instrument_protocol: Six wearables compared with ECG across rest and activities; device model activity context timestamps algorithm version and missingness required
missingness: Full text explicitly analyzed device missing values; exact rates not extracted
effect_estimate: No significant accuracy difference by skin tone; significant device and activity differences; mean absolute error during activity about 30% higher than rest
limitations: Not runner- or youth-specific; devices and algorithms age quickly; PPG HR does not transfer to distance elevation energy expenditure fatigue or readiness; model or algorithm changes create continuity boundaries

intervention: Seven wrist-worn devices measuring heart rate and vendor energy expenditure
comparator: Telemetry for heart rate and indirect calorimetry for energy expenditure
component_dose: Internal response proxy: heart rate in beats per minute; derived vendor energy expenditure in energy units; external activity condition; device model participant and protocol provenance retained separately
component_order: NOT_APPLICABLE
outcome_measure: Heart-rate error and energy-expenditure error by device activity and participant factors
instrument_protocol: Seven watches during sitting walking running and cycling; telemetry and indirect-calorimetry criteria; model activity algorithm and outcome provenance required
time_points: Sitting walking running and cycling conditions
effect_estimate: Six devices had median HR error below 5% during cycling; no device had energy expenditure error below 20%; error varied by activity device and participant factors
uncertainty: Median error summaries; exact confidence intervals not reported in abstract extraction
limitations: Adult laboratory sample with older models; cycling HR cannot be assumed for running; vendor energy is algorithm-derived; HR and energy outcomes are non-equivalent and cannot be merged into fatigue readiness or one load total

intervention: Positioning-enabled watch distance measurement
comparator: Reference route distance 404.0 to 4296.9 metres
component_dose: External work: distance in metres or kilometres; contextual provenance: watch model environment activity firmware or algorithm and route protocol; error retained as percent
component_order: NOT_APPLICABLE
outcome_measure: Distance error MAPE and underestimation by model environment and activity
instrument_protocol: Eight Apple Coros Garmin Polar and Suunto watches; 3 x 12 measurements in urban forest and track settings during walking running and cycling; reference route and device version required
time_points: Urban forest and track conditions across three activities
effect_estimate: MAPE ranged 3.2% to 6.1%; distance could be underestimated up to 9%; urban forest and running were generally less accurate
limitations: Instrument rather than athlete-population validation; small repeated routes; model firmware environment and activity specific; post-algorithm series cannot be silently merged; distance error carries no fatigue or readiness meaning

intervention: Garmin activity-tracker outputs across models
comparator: Study-specific validity criteria and repeated reliability where available
component_dose: External outputs: steps distance speed elevation in original units; internal proxy: heart rate in beats per minute; derived energy expenditure in energy units; model outcome protocol and version provenance retained
component_order: NOT_APPLICABLE
outcome_measure: Steps distance energy expenditure heart rate speed elevation validity; reliability
instrument_protocol: Systematic review of 32 studies and multiple models; only four reliability studies; registry must use model outcome protocol and version rather than brand-only identity
effect_estimate: Steps generally performed best; distance poor in three studies; energy expenditure widely variable; HR low-to-excellent; speed and elevation evidence sparse
uncertainty: Heterogeneous study-specific estimates not pooled here
limitations: Heterogeneous older devices and adult samples; few reliability studies; review thresholds are not universal cutoffs; brand-level validity is invalid; no cross-outcome summation fatigue or readiness inference

population: 93 high-school basketball players; 45 female and 48 male
intervention: Ten single or machine-learning imputation methods plus multiple imputation of actual missingness
comparator: Observed jumps per hour and RPE values masked then reconstructed; simple and context-aware methods compared
component_dose: External work: jumps per hour with count and duration units; internal response: RPE points; imputed values are separate versioned derivatives with model predictors and uncertainty; canonical observations unchanged
component_order: Session and athlete context used by some methods; no parent-to-leaf allocation
outcome_measure: Imputation error or performance under simulated and actual missingness
instrument_protocol: One-season longitudinal monitoring; masking mechanism imputation method version predictors training data timestamps and uncertainty required
missingness: Observed values were masked for simulation and actual missingness was multiply imputed; amount and mechanism determine method choice
effect_estimate: Team-session mean was outperformed only by methods combining session and individual context in some analyses
uncertainty: Method-specific uncertainty not extracted from abstract
individual_response: Individual context used by better-performing methods; individual errors not extracted
limitations: Basketball and jump counts rather than running; simulated missingness may differ from real mechanisms; analytic imputation cannot overwrite or masquerade as observation; missing is not zero and imputation cannot create fatigue readiness or clearance

population: Illustrative American-football wearable data; exact cohort not verified
intervention: Methodological analysis of missing wearable workload data and potential imputation
comparator: Missing-completely-at-random assumption versus informative equipment-failure and noncompliance mechanisms
component_dose: External or device-derived workload remains in reported units; contextual provenance: eligible observed and missing counts reason device protocol; any imputation remains separate versioned derivative
component_order: NOT_APPLICABLE
outcome_measure: Coverage eligible count observed count missing reason and potential dashboard bias
instrument_protocol: Didactic wearable example; missingness mechanism denominator eligibility imputation method and uncertainty must be explicit
time_horizon: Longitudinal monitoring example; exact duration not verified
missingness: Equipment failure and noncompliance can create informative missingness; exact missing rates not verified
effect_estimate: Even simple dashboard averages can be biased when missingness assumptions fail
limitations: Didactic example not youth runners; recommendations concern analysis not canonical truth; never zero-fill or silently drop missing records; no imputed readiness fatigue or prescription state

intervention: W3C PROV-O ontology for interoperable provenance
comparator: Heterogeneous systems and their source entities activities and agents
component_dose: Contextual provenance: entity activity agent generation usage derivation attribution revision and time; every internal external or derived measure retains quantity unit method and lineage
component_order: Parent session and ordered leaf entities linked by explicit derivation and membership; revisions create new entities rather than double counting or overwrite
outcome_measure: Entity activity agent and wasDerivedFrom lineage
instrument_protocol: Official W3C Recommendation; versioned generating activity source entities responsible software or user agent timestamps and revision identity required
effect_estimate: Defines interoperable provenance relations; no sport effect estimate
limitations: General data standard not sport-science validation; does not prescribe TRAINORACLE session graph physiological meaning fatigue or readiness; parent and leaf accounting rule remains a conservative product constraint

intervention: Conceptual and statistical critique of acute-to-chronic workload ratios
comparator: Published ACWR variants and injury-association interpretations
component_dose: Derived ratio only: compatible numerator and denominator loads retain their original quantity unit and method; ratio provenance includes acute and chronic windows coupling weighting missing-day and zero-denominator behavior
component_order: NOT_APPLICABLE
separation_hours: Method-specific rolling windows; not a session separation rule
outcome_measure: Ratio artifacts ambiguity causal interpretation and direction inconsistency
instrument_protocol: Critical methodological commentary; full formula version numerator denominator windows coupling weighting and raw compatible inputs required
missingness: Missing-day behavior must be declared for any descriptive ratio
effect_estimate: Identifies ratio artifacts ambiguity lack of causal estimation and inconsistent direction; rejects training recommendations from injury associations
limitations: Commentary does not make every descriptive ratio arithmetically useless; no risk zone plan action fatigue readiness injury prediction or training clearance; default off

population: Hypothetical 1000-player squad based on elite Australian-football distributions
intervention: Coupled conventional ACWR calculation
comparator: Uncoupled acute-excluded-from-chronic calculation
component_dose: External high-speed distance across four independent 7-day periods retained in distance units; derived ACWR is a dimensionless named ratio with window coupling and weighting provenance
component_order: Four independent weekly periods; ratio parent references raw period inputs without replacing them
separation_hours: Seven-day acute and chronic-period structure; not recovery separation
outcome_measure: Coupled and uncoupled correlations and example ratios
instrument_protocol: Simulation based on elite Australian-football distributions; formula windows input series and coupling identity required
time_points: Each simulated 7-day period and derived ratio
effect_estimate: Coupled acute/chronic correlation r=.52 despite independent weeks versus uncoupled r=.01; example ratio 1.45 coupled versus 1.71 uncoupled
uncertainty: 95% CI .47 to .56 for coupled r; -.05 to .07 for uncoupled r
individual_response: 1,000 simulated players; not observed individual response
limitations: Simulation with no athlete outcome; observed-data counterevidence found smaller practical differences; variants are non-identical but neither gains universal privilege; no fatigue readiness risk zone or plan action

intervention: Coupled and uncoupled rolling-average ACWR variants
comparator: Coupled and uncoupled exponentially weighted moving-average variants
component_dose: Underlying workload retains method and unit; derived ACWR dimensionless series retain acute and chronic windows coupling weighting missingness and denominator provenance
component_order: Sport-specific time series; no component split
separation_hours: Method-specific rolling windows; exact windows not extracted
outcome_measure: Effect sizes and correlations between calculation variants
instrument_protocol: Observed elite-sport case datasets; every ratio formula and raw compatible input series required
time_horizon: Olympic-qualifying preparation; exact duration not reported
effect_estimate: Coupled-versus-uncoupled effect sizes mostly g=.04-.21; variant correlations r=.88-.99
individual_response: Sport-specific series; individual responses not reported
limitations: Small elite case context; no runner or youth directness; trivial differences do not establish formula identity; neither coupling variant becomes universal; no fatigue readiness risk or prescription meaning

intervention: Narrative ACWR model with proposed approximate 0.8 to 1.3 sweet spot
comparator: Prior training-load and injury evidence summarized narratively
component_dose: Underlying load units and methods heterogeneous; derived dimensionless ACWR requires named windows coupling weighting and inputs; proposed bands are contextual historical claims only
component_order: NOT_APPLICABLE
separation_hours: Method-specific rolling windows; not session recovery intervals
outcome_measure: Narrative injury association and proposed workload ratio band
instrument_protocol: Narrative/model paper; source methods formula choices and unpublished components require explicit provenance
adverse_events: Injury outcomes reported narratively; not accepted as RQ-D product outputs
effect_estimate: Proposes monitoring recent relative to chronic workload and an approximate 0.8-1.3 sweet spot
limitations: Narrative not intervention trial; team-sport indirectness; figure includes unpublished data; contradicted or unsupported by D22-D24; zones must not enter descriptive accounting fatigue readiness risk or plan action

intervention: Coupled uncoupled and EWMA ACWR using 2-week acute over 4 6 8 10 or 12-week chronic hours or kilometres
comparator: Comparison among calculation variants and running-related injury observations
component_dose: External running load: hours and kilometres kept separate; derived dimensionless ACWR keeps load unit acute/chronic windows coupling weighting and biweekly sampling provenance
component_order: Biweekly aggregate series; hours and kilometres are not added or converted without a defined method
separation_hours: Two-week acute and 4 6 8 10 or 12-week chronic windows; not a session recovery interval
outcome_measure: ACWR variants and observed running-related injury probability
instrument_protocol: Biweekly exposure with Bayesian logistic mixed models; raw unit window coupling weighting and sampling cadence required
missingness: EWMA results were sparse; exact missing-data handling not extracted
adverse_events: Running-related injuries observed but not accepted as descriptive-load output or causal target
effect_estimate: Higher ACWR associated with lower observed injury probability; coupled and uncoupled similar; EWMA sparse and non-significant
uncertainty: Bayesian model uncertainty not fully extracted
limitations: Secondary observational adult-runner analysis; association not causal risk or product rule; hours and kilometres are distinct series; no universal threshold fatigue readiness or plan action

population: 482 elite Norwegian U19 football players across 34 teams
intervention: Load-management intervention using coupled 7-day over 28-day rolling ACWR and target 0.8 to 1.5
comparator: Usual planning
component_dose: Underlying external workload retains source quantity and unit; derived dimensionless ACWR carries 7-day numerator 28-day denominator coupled rolling method target band missingness and adherence provenance
component_order: Team and player records retained; ratio cannot replace component sessions
separation_hours: 7-day acute over 28-day chronic window; not recovery separation
outcome_measure: Health-problem prevalence and relative risk under ACWR-guided management
instrument_protocol: Cluster randomized trial; coupled rolling formula target band response adherence and raw input provenance required
time_points: Repeated season monitoring and health reports
missingness: Incomplete response and adherence reported qualitatively; exact rates not extracted here
adverse_events: Health problems were the trial outcome but are outside accepted RQ-D product outputs
effect_estimate: Prevalence difference 1.8 percentage points; RR 1.01; p=.55; no intervention benefit
uncertainty: Prevalence-difference interval -4.1 to 7.7; RR interval .91 to 1.12
individual_response: Clustered team-level intervention; individual response not reported
limitations: Football and one implementation; incomplete response and adherence; does not test descriptive display; ACWR zones gain no plan authority fatigue readiness safety or clearance meaning

intervention: Weekly load-change threshold and coupled uncoupled and EWMA ACWR methods
comparator: Classification agreement across four calculation methods
component_dose: External running distance retained in original distance units across 22,839 GPS sessions; derived dimensionless ratios and percent-change flags retain window threshold coupling weighting GPS and missingness provenance
component_order: Weekly aggregate references raw sessions; no parent and leaf double count
separation_hours: Weekly and method-specific acute/chronic windows; exact nonweekly windows not extracted here
outcome_measure: Flagged-session proportion and disagreement across weekly coupled uncoupled and EWMA methods
instrument_protocol: GPS-derived session distance; threshold formula window coupling weighting device source and raw input lineage required
time_horizon: 22,839 sessions; study duration not reported here
effect_estimate: Flagged 33.4% weekly 16.2% coupled 25.8% uncoupled and 18.9% EWMA; 43.0% of weekly flags differed from coupled or EWMA classifications
limitations: Predefined unvalidated thresholds; adult runners; distance only; descriptive differences do not establish correctness; default no ACWR classification and no fatigue readiness risk or action recommendation
